bead.title: Add end-to-end snapshot autorouting fixtures
bead.description:
Add end-to-end fixture coverage that proves the combined snapshot-first autorouting contract across CLI/service/routing surfaces.

Scenarios to cover:
- cheap chooses nano/mini/local over gpt-5.5 when sufficient.
- default chooses the cheapest routine-sufficient candidate, not frontier, when lower-cost candidates are healthy.
- smart can select gpt-5.5 when quality dominates.
- no_remote forces local selection or a clear constraint failure when all locals are down.
- metered opt-out rejects actual-cash-spend pay-per-token providers; opt-in lets them win if utility is best.
- exact model pin selects the pinned model or fails clearly when undispatchable.
- stale unknown health is refreshed or penalized according to snapshot freshness state; known-down is excluded.
- repeated fiz models does not create refresh storms.

In scope: fixture snapshots, integration-style tests, CLI golden JSON where useful.

Out of scope: large live-provider tests; this bead should be deterministic/offline.
bead.acceptance:
1. go test ./... -run 'Test.*SnapshotAutorouting|Test.*EffectiveCostRouting|Test.*NoRemote|Test.*MeteredOptIn|Test.*RefreshStorm' -count=1 passes.
2. Test fixtures live under an appropriate testdata directory and contain no live credentials.
3. At least one test replays a route decision using only fixture snapshot plus client inputs and asserts no provider call occurs.
4. At least one CLI-level test exercises fiz models stale behavior and route-status JSON evidence from fixtures.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
